page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the arm64 architecture, the processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates exceptional memory performance, with the PassMark memory score placing the instance in the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block cached speeds of 462.16 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds of 110.92 GB/sec. General CPU performance is average, as reflected by a Geekbench single-core score of 1820.0 and a multi-core score of 5893.0. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in cryptographic and extended instruction tests, scoring in the poor tier for PassMark CPU encryption due to the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets.

The r8gn.xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or specialized x86 instructions. It is highly suited for database operations, as demonstrated by a Redis SET performance of 8.54 million operations per second and average PassMark database operation scores. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, memory-intensive caching layers, and general-purpose ARM-native applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture at 2.8 GHz, it provides 4 dedicated physical cores and 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware but features a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and a Redis SET throughput of 8.54 million operations per second. Conversely, CPU-bound cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks are weak due to the absence of AVX and AMX support. This profile makes the r8gn.xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and static web serving, while presenting a clear tradeoff for compute-heavy or cryptographic tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server with 4 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Graviton processors and high-bandwidth memory for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Top-tier memory performance with 462.16 GB/sec cached read bandwidth.
Baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Lacks local storage and specialized x86 vector instructions (AVX/AMX).
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications.
